DEFENDANTS CINDY LEUNG AND JOHN OUYANG'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS JOSE LUIS RAMIREZ VARGAS AND LESLIE MONICA JIMENEZ'S RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE IS GRANTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-05-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS CINDY LEUNG, AND JOHN OUYANG's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Cindy Leung and John Ouyang's unopposed motion to compel plaintiffs Jose Luis Ramirez Vargas and Leslie Monica Jimenez's responses to its Request for Production of Documents, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One; and Special Interrogatories, Set One is granted. Plaintiffs failed to timely respond to this discovery and have thereby waived objections. (CCP 2031.300(a); 2030.290(a).) Plaintiffs are ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order.

Defendants also seek sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." A comparable provision covers sanctions where a party must move to compel interrogatory responses. (CCP 2030.290(c).) The court does not find that plaintiffs acted with substantial justification and awards $573.57 in sanctions, payable by Plaintiffs, to Defendants within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Set For Trial On Feb-09-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
